title: 控方证人
summary:
While traveling by train, a man named Raoul Le Verte joins a conversation between a doctor, Canon, and Sir George Durand regarding the case of Felicity Bault. Le Verte reveals that he was raised in a home run by a philanthropist named Miss Slade, alongside Felicie Boulanger and Annette Lavelle (also referred to as Annette Ravil). He explains that understanding Annette’s character is essential to understanding Felicity.

Le Verte describes Annette as a passionate, commanding girl who frequently bullied the physically strong but "stupid" Felicity. While Felicity resented Annette’s mockery and insults regarding her parentage, she continued to follow Annette. Le Verte recounts an incident where Annette used an early book on hypnosis to manipulate Felicity. By having Felicity stare at a spinning brass ball, Annette successfully commanded her to eat a candle in a playground, claiming it was the best cake she had ever tasted. The prank caused Felicity to experience confusion and intense hatred toward Annette, though Annette continued to dominate her.

As they grew older, Annette’s ambitions shifted from dancing to singing. Under the guidance of Miss Slade, Annette trained intensely as a singer. Le Verte notes that Miss Slade eventually expressed concern regarding Annette's health, specifically a persistent cough resulting from her excessive practice.
